title: frangula purshiana subsp. purshiana
intro: Frangula purshiana subsp. purshiana, also known as Cascara Sagrada, is a plant species native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, belonging to the buckthorn family. The medicinal properties of Cascara Sagrada lie in its bark, which contains anthraquinone glycosides, specifically aloe-emodin-8-O-β-D-glucopyranoside, that have been used to support digestive health. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve constipation, irritable bowel syndrome, and other gastrointestinal issues due to its laxative properties, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Cascara Sagrada can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, making it a popular natural remedy among those seeking to maintain a healthy digestive system.
